description: Merge-aware Decisions and DB Freshness — replace branch-scoped user-decision tracking with a project-wide decisions table indexed by description hash; make `seshat serve` and `seshat review` detect git state changes (branch label OR HEAD movement) on startup and trigger incremental sync; add `seshat decisions` CLI subcommand; continue working without git via a synthetic single-branch fallback.

userStories.title: V11 + V12 migrations and repo skeletons
userStories.description: As a developer, I need new migration files in place before any consumer can be migrated, so that downstream stories have schema to compile against.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/seshat-storage/migrations/V11__branches_table.sql creates the branches table with branch_id PRIMARY KEY, last_scanned_commit TEXT, last_scanned_at INTEGER, snapshot_source TEXT, created_at INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (unixepoch())

crates/seshat-storage/migrations/V12__decisions_table.sql creates the decisions table per the PRD schema (description_hash PK, state CHECK, nature CHECK, weight CHECK, plus indexes idx_decisions_state and idx_decisions_decided_on_branch)
Empty decision_repository.rs and branch_repository.rs extension stubs compile
cargo build --workspace passes
cargo fmt --all -- --check passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Implemented on feat/merge-aware-decisions. V11 + V12 SQL migrations created; DecisionRepository trait + SqliteDecisionRepository skeleton (unimplemented! bodies for US-002 to fill); BranchRepository extended with default-impl stubs for get_last_scanned_commit/set_last_scanned_commit/ensure_branch_exists (US-003 overrides). cargo build/fmt/clippy/test all green.

userStories.title: DecisionRepository implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a fully-tested decisions repository so that all consumers can read/write decisions through one API.
acceptanceCriteria: DecisionRepository trait with methods: upsert, get_by_hash, get_by_hashes (bulk), delete, count_by_state, list, list_by_state
SqliteDecisionRepository implementation
Decision struct (PRD §Design — schema) implements Debug + Clone + PartialEq
DecisionState, DecisionNature, DecisionWeight enums implemented
Unit tests cover: empty table, single insert, upsert-replaces-on-conflict, bulk lookup with mixed-found/missing hashes, count by state filter, delete idempotency, list ordering
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN on get_by_hash shows index lookup, not scan
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Implemented SqliteDecisionRepository on top of V12 schema. All 7 trait methods (upsert / get_by_hash / get_by_hashes / delete / count_by_state / list / list_by_state) wired with chunked IN-clause bulk lookup (≤500 params/chunk per PRD §US-008). 13 unit tests cover empty table, upsert round-trip, upsert-replaces-on-conflict, bulk lookup mixed-found/missing, bulk chunking above 500 hashes, count by state, delete idempotency, list ordering by decided_at DESC, list_by_state filter+order, EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN asserting SEARCH (not SCAN), enum SQL round-trip, examples JSON round-trip, nullable columns. cargo build/fmt/clippy/test all green.

userStories.title: BranchRepository extensions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need per-branch metadata persisted in the branches table so that freshness checks have a sentinel to compare against.
acceptanceCriteria: get_last_scanned_commit(&BranchId) -> Result<Option<String>> reads from branches
set_last_scanned_commit(&BranchId, &str) -> Result<()> upserts branches and also writes last_scanned_at = unixepoch()
ensure_branch_exists(&BranchId) -> Result<()> is idempotent INSERT OR IGNORE
list_branches is rewritten to SELECT branch_id FROM branches ORDER BY branch_id (no longer SELECT DISTINCT branch_id FROM nodes)
create_snapshot(source, target) also INSERTs into branches with snapshot_source = source.0
Unit test list_branches_reads_from_branches_table_not_nodes verifies the regression cannot return
Unit test for set_last_scanned_commit upsert behaviour (overwrites previous value)
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Promoted the three skeleton methods from default-unimplemented to required trait methods and implemented them on SqliteBranchRepository. list_branches rewritten to read from `branches` table. create_snapshot now ensures source exists and UPSERTs target with snapshot_source set, in the same transaction as the data copy. delete_branch drops the branches registry row last. switch_branch implicitly ensures branch existence so existing consumers continue to surface in list_branches. scan_project_with_progress calls ensure_branch_exists at scan start (US-009 will layer set_last_scanned_commit on top). 11 new unit tests cover ensure idempotency, ensure not clobbering existing metadata, get_last_scanned_commit None for unknown/unscanned branches, set round-trip, upsert-overwrite (PRD-mandated regression guard), monotonic last_scanned_at, snapshot registers source+target with snapshot_source, delete removes registry row, switch implicit registration, and the regression guard list_branches_reads_from_branches_table_not_nodes. cargo build/fmt/clippy/test all green.

userStories.title: Update persist_conventions auto-scan dedup
userStories.description: As a user, when I rescan after deciding on conventions, the decided ones must not be re-inserted as auto-detected.
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE FROM nodes WHERE branch_id=?1 AND source='auto_detected' no longer carries the user_rejected exception (rejections live in decisions now)
Pre-compute description_hash for all aggregated conventions
Bulk-fetch decisions rows with description_hash IN (...) in a single query (chunked to ≤500 params per SELECT)
For each aggregated convention with a matching decision: skip the INSERT regardless of state
Auto-detected nodes are inserted with description_hash populated (column kept on nodes for the JOIN in US-006)
Unit test: 100 conventions, 50 with matching decisions in any state, 50 inserted
Unit test regression: bulk path issues exactly 1 SELECT against decisions, not N
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Rewrote persist_conventions to be decisions-aware. (1) DELETE clause no longer carries the user_rejected exception — every auto_detected node is unconditionally deleted at the start of each persist; rejections now live in the decisions table and are honoured at INSERT time. (2) New private helper bulk_fetch_decided_hashes(conn, &hashes) computes description_hash for ALL aggregated conventions up front and resolves them via SqliteDecisionRepository::get_by_hashes (chunked at 500/select). The bulk fetch runs BEFORE acquiring the persist transaction lock to avoid lock-churn during the write window. (3) The per-iteration nodes-table SELECT for user_duplicate is gone. (4) INSERT loop skips any aggregated convention whose hash is in the decided set, regardless of state, and populates the description_hash column on every insert. Tests: removed obsolete persist_conventions_skips_user_rejected and persist_conventions_skips_user_rejected_integration; added persist_conventions_no_longer_preserves_user_rejected_nodes (regression: exception removed), persist_conventions_skips_inserts_with_matching_decision_in_any_state (AC: 100 conventions / 50 decided across all 4 states / 50 inserted / description_hash populated on all inserts), persist_conventions_bulk_decision_lookup_uses_single_select (AC: trace_v2 capture asserts exactly 1 SELECT against decisions for 100 conventions), and run_detection_cycle_skips_conventions_with_matching_decision_in_decisions_table (end-to-end through run_detection_cycle). Added the `trace` feature to the workspace rusqlite dep so tests can use trace_v2. cargo build/fmt/clippy/test all green.

userStories.title: TUI confirm / reject / partial migrate to DecisionRepository
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the TUI review flow writing to the new table so that approvals propagate cross-branch.
acceptanceCriteria: confirm_convention (in seshat-cli/src/tui/app.rs) computes description_hash and UPSERTs decisions with state='approved', decided_on_branch=current, examples serialised as JSON; stops calling record_decision
reject_convention UPSERTs decisions with state='rejected'; the auto-detected node DELETE step still runs (cosmetic; cleaner snapshot output)
partial_convention UPSERTs decisions with state='partial'; the preference node creation is dropped (preference rows now live in decisions with state='partial')
Optimistic concurrency check (expected_hash) continues to operate on the auto-detected node's ext_data snapshot — the user-decided row is keyed by hash so collisions are not possible
Existing tui_review_integration.rs tests are updated to assert decision rows in decisions, not user nodes in nodes
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in 2026-05-06. confirm_convention/reject_convention/partial_convention now UPSERT into the decisions table via SqliteDecisionRepository keyed by compute_description_hash. State='approved' for confirms, 'rejected' for rejects, 'partial' for partials. The auto-detected node soft-delete (removed=1) is preserved for cleaner snapshot output. Optimistic concurrency check on ext_data snapshot remains; the user-decided row is keyed by hash so collisions are not possible.

userStories.title: Update query_conventions_for_review with LEFT JOIN against decisions
userStories.description: As a user, I should not see conventions in the review queue that I have already decided on (any state).
acceptanceCriteria: Query rewritten as LEFT JOIN decisions d ON d.description_hash = n.description_hash WHERE d.id IS NULL AND n.nature IN ('convention','observation') AND n.branch_id = ?1
No usage of ext_data->>'source' or ext_data->>'user_rejected' remains in the review query
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN confirms index usage on decisions(description_hash)
Unit test: insert two auto nodes, decide one (any state), verify only the undecided one returns
Unit test: bulk case — 100 auto nodes, 50 decided across all four states, verify only 50 returned
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Rewrote query_conventions_for_review in seshat-cli/src/tui/app.rs as a LEFT JOIN against the V12 decisions table, keyed by description_hash. The PRD AC text reads `WHERE d.id IS NULL` but V12 has no id column — the PK is description_hash itself, so the predicate is `d.description_hash IS NULL` (LEFT JOIN semantics: unmatched rows have NULL on the d side). Dropped both `ext_data->>'source' != 'user'` and `ext_data->>'user_rejected' != 1` filters and the in-nodes-table self-join sub-SELECT. SQL_NOT_REMOVED stays as the soft-delete filter. Added 4 unit tests (excludes_decided_node small case, excludes_decided_in_any_state bulk case spanning all 4 states, uses_index_on_decisions EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN guard, keeps_undecided_with_null_hash defensive guard for legacy NULL-hash rows). Added end-to-end LEFT-JOIN assertion to existing query_conventions_excludes_user_rejected integration test. Also patched setup_empty_db in tui/mod.rs to add a decisions table fixture so the query resolves cleanly there too. cargo build/fmt/clippy/test all green.

userStories.title: Update count_confirmed_conventions to read from decisions table
userStories.description: As a user, the review header shows the project-wide count of approved conventions, not branch-scoped.
acceptanceCriteria: count_confirmed_conventions becomes SELECT COUNT(*) FROM decisions WHERE state IN ('approved','partial','recorded')
No branch_id filter
Unit tests cover empty table, mixed states, large count
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Rewrote count_confirmed_conventions in seshat-cli/src/tui/app.rs to query the V12 decisions table: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM decisions WHERE state IN ('approved', 'partial', 'recorded')`. Rejected rows are excluded — they're decisions, but they don't represent confirmed conventions. The branch_id parameter was dropped from the function signature (the AC mandates 'no branch_id filter'); the single caller in tui/mod.rs was updated. Doc comment rewritten to describe the new project-wide semantics. Tests: kept count_confirmed_conventions_returns_zero_on_empty_db (empty-table case, AC-required), added 3 new tests — count_confirmed_conventions_counts_only_approved_partial_recorded (mixed-states case: 2 approved + 1 partial + 2 recorded + 2 rejected = 5 counted), count_confirmed_conventions_ignores_branch_filter (project-wide regression guard: 3 decisions on 3 different branches all count), count_confirmed_conventions_handles_large_count (large-count case: 250 confirmed across the 3 confirmed-counting states + 50 rejected; expects 250). cargo build/fmt/clippy/test all green.

userStories.title: Update existing tests to new decisions schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests that reference the old user-node + ext_data schema to be migrated to the new model so CI stays green.
acceptanceCriteria: All assertions in crates/seshat-detectors/tests/snippet_quality.rs that check ext_data.source = 'user' or description_hash on nodes are updated to query decisions
All assertions in crates/seshat-cli/tests/tui_review_integration.rs are updated similarly
Any INSERT INTO nodes test fixture that relied on source='user' for user-decision testing is rewritten to insert into decisions
Full workspace cargo test --workspace passes (CI green)
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Verification checkpoint — the migration work for the AC patterns was rolled into earlier stories that shipped each schema change with its tests. Audit results: (1) crates/seshat-detectors/tests/snippet_quality.rs has zero references to ext_data.source, 'user', user_rejected, description_hash, or INSERT INTO nodes — it is a pure detector-pipeline test (no DB) and the AC is vacuously satisfied. (2) crates/seshat-cli/tests/tui_review_integration.rs has zero `ext_data.source = 'user'` or `description_hash on nodes` assertions; description_hash references in this file are calls to compute_description_hash() used as keys for the new decisions table (correct for the new model), and the only ext_data references are for compute_snapshot_hash() which is the optimistic-concurrency check on auto-detected nodes (correct per US-005 AC). The file was migrated by US-005's commit (assertions against SqliteDecisionRepository::get_by_hash now pin the new state-of-truth). (3) No INSERT INTO nodes test fixtures with source='user' for user-decision testing exist in any tests/ directory (workspace-wide grep across **/tests/**/*.rs returned zero matches). Inline #[cfg(test)] tests in seshat-graph/src/decisions.rs that still reference ext_data.source = 'user' are testing the legacy seshat_graph::record_decision (and update_decision/remove_decision) function, which is owned by US-004 — those tests will migrate alongside the production function. The integration test record_decision_creates_new_node in tui_review_integration.rs:278 falls in the same bucket — it tests the legacy seshat_graph::record_decision function via find_conventions_by_branch and is left to US-004 to migrate when the function's contract changes. cargo test --workspace passes (2,512+ tests, zero failures). cargo fmt --all -- --check and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings both pass.

userStories.title: Migrate MCP record_decision, update_decision, remove_decision to DecisionRepository
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the MCP decision tools writing to the new table so that explicit user decisions and TUI approvals share one source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: seshat_mcp::tools::record_decision writes to decisions with state='recorded'; no node is created
seshat_mcp::tools::update_decision updates existing row in decisions (description, reason, examples, weight)
seshat_mcp::tools::remove_decision deletes from decisions
All INSERT INTO nodes ... SQL inside these tool implementations is removed
Existing MCP tool tests are updated to assert against the decisions table
query_convention and query_project_context MCP tools read from decisions (state='recorded' rows + state='approved'/'partial' rows count toward project knowledge)
No regression in MCP envelope shape — query_* response JSON keeps the same fields, just sourced from a different table
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Rewrote seshat_graph::record_decision/update_decision/remove_decision to operate on the V12 decisions table via SqliteDecisionRepository. record_decision builds a Decision { state: Recorded, ... } and upserts it (no nodes INSERT, no FTS5 entry). update_decision does get_by_hash, merges fields, re-upserts (PK preserved even when description changes). remove_decision is now a hard delete (matches the AC text 'deletes from decisions' — no soft-delete in the new schema). NotUserDecision check is gone: all decisions table rows are user-recorded by definition; missing rows return NodeNotFound. Public identity changed from rowid (i64) to description_hash (String): RecordDecisionData/UpdateDecisionData/RemoveDecisionData all return description_hash; UpdateDecisionParams/RemoveDecisionParams take description_hash; MCP UpdateDecisionRequest/RemoveDecisionRequest schema renamed id:i64 → description_hash:String; metadata.node_id renamed to metadata.description_hash; tracing fields use description_hash. The MCP envelope shape change is scoped to the mutation tools (AC #7's 'no regression' is explicitly scoped to query_*). query_convention now searches BOTH the FTS5 nodes index AND the decisions table (LIKE-based keyword search filtered to states approved/partial/recorded) and merges deduplicated by description_hash with decisions taking precedence. query_project_context's query_conventions helper splits into query_node_conventions (existing nodes path) + query_decision_conventions (new decisions path) and concatenates so conventions_count includes recorded/approved/partial decisions. ConventionResult gains an additive description_hash field (skip_serializing_if=String::is_empty so legacy rows don't bloat the envelope). DecisionEntry in validate_approach gains description_hash; doc comments updated to point at description_hash for update/remove. call_logger::decision_result(node_id: i64) became decision_result(description_hash: &str). Inline tests in seshat-graph/src/decisions.rs fully rewritten (10 tests) covering: writes to decisions with state=Recorded, no user-source nodes leak, examples persist as JSON, invalid nature/weight, case-insensitive nature/weight, upsert on repeat for same hash, modify description/nature/weight/category/examples/reason, PK preserved when description changes (callers must remove+re-record to re-key), hash-not-found returns NodeNotFound for both update and remove, hard delete removes row, end-to-end visibility via query_convention, decision survives auto_detected re-scan dedup. MCP tool tests updated (record_decision/update_decision/remove_decision tools, server.rs end-to-end tests). The integration test record_decision_creates_new_node (renamed to record_decision_writes_to_decisions_table_not_nodes) now asserts the V12 row is created AND that no user-source nodes leak — closing the open chicken-and-egg from US-018's audit. cargo build/fmt/clippy/test all green; 2,502 tests pass workspace-wide, zero failures.

userStories.title: Wire last_scanned_commit updates in scan paths
userStories.description: As the system, I record the git HEAD at scan-completion time so that subsequent startups can detect divergence.
acceptanceCriteria: scan_project (in seshat-cli/src/scan.rs) reads git rev-parse HEAD and calls branch_repo.set_last_scanned_commit(branch_id, head) AFTER successful scan + persist
background_sync (in seshat-cli/src/serve.rs) does the same at end of run, regardless of diff/fallback path
fallback_rescan does the same
execute_bulk_rescan (in seshat-watcher) does the same
run_detection_cycle_sync invocations triggered by warm-tier do the same
Git-unavailable: skipped silently with debug log; column stays NULL
Integration test: scan succeeds → branches.last_scanned_commit matches git rev-parse HEAD for the active branch
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Implemented two new helpers in seshat-scanner/src/git_utils.rs: (1) get_head_commit(path) reads `git rev-parse HEAD` via gix (Some on success, None when path is not a git repo / has no commits / gix open fails); (2) record_branch_scan_complete(branch_repo, root, branch_id) is the canonical post-scan hook — it reads HEAD via get_head_commit and calls set_last_scanned_commit on success, debug-logs and no-ops on git-unavailable (column stays NULL per the AC). Storage errors are warn-logged and swallowed so a successful scan is not regressed by a freshness-sentinel write failure. Wired into 4 production paths: run_scan in seshat-cli/src/scan.rs (root + each parallel submodule scan), background_sync in seshat-cli/src/serve.rs (after diff sync + detection), fallback_rescan in seshat-cli/src/serve.rs (after rescan + detection), execute_bulk_rescan in seshat-watcher/src/lib.rs (after scan + detection, regardless of detection-cycle outcome — IR was already re-synced to HEAD). AC#5 ("run_detection_cycle_sync invocations triggered by warm-tier") is vacuously satisfied: a workspace-wide audit finds the only callers of run_detection_cycle_sync are background_sync, fallback_rescan, and execute_bulk_rescan (all instrumented above); the watcher's start_warm_tier task uses run_detection_cycle directly (no _sync) and only re-runs detection on existing IR — no scan completion happens there, so updating last_scanned_commit would be a lie. Integration test in seshat-cli/tests/scan_records_head.rs covers both AC paths: scan_project_records_last_scanned_commit_for_active_branch (real git fixture, asserts stored == git rev-parse HEAD) and scan_project_records_no_commit_when_git_unavailable (no-git tmp dir, asserts column stays NULL). 4 new unit tests in seshat-scanner/src/git_utils.rs lock the helpers themselves: returns_none_for_non_git_directory + returns_none_for_nonexistent_path (existing tests now also exercise the new get_head_commit alongside get_submodule_commit_hash), get_head_commit_returns_hash_for_real_git_repo (asserts gix HEAD matches git rev-parse HEAD on a fresh fixture, replacing the previous test that walked up to the workspace .git), record_branch_scan_complete_writes_head_to_branches_table, record_branch_scan_complete_is_silent_noop_when_git_unavailable. cargo build --workspace, cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings all green; cargo test --workspace --exclude seshat-bin all green. The pre-existing flaky serve_starts_and_shows_startup_info test in seshat-bin (dangerous-cwd guardrail trips on macOS tmpfs) reproduces on clean main with no US-009 patches applied — confirmed unrelated.

userStories.title: HEAD-change detection in run_serve
userStories.description: As a user, when I git pull on the same branch and restart Claude Code, seshat detects the new HEAD and rescans.
acceptanceCriteria: After handle_branch_switch returns final_branch, run_serve reads last_scanned_commit for that branch
Computes git rev-parse HEAD; if git unavailable, skip the comparison and trigger no sync
needs_sync = sync_old_branch.is_some() || (last_scanned_commit != current_head)
If needs_sync, spawn background_sync with the OLD commit hint (used for gix tree-diff if reachable; otherwise fallback_rescan)
Logs include old_head=<7-char>, new_head=<7-char> so the user can see what triggered the sync
Integration test: with last_scanned_commit=abc123, set git rev-parse HEAD to def456 (via fixture), start serve, assert background_sync was triggered
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Added a new freshness-check helper `seshat_scanner::check_branch_freshness(branch_repo, root, branch_id) -> FreshnessCheck` (in `crates/seshat-scanner/src/git_utils.rs`, re-exported from `lib.rs`). The enum has three variants: `UpToDate` (sentinel matches HEAD), `GitUnavailable` (no .git / empty repo / gix open failed — AC#2's git-unavailable case), and `Stale { old_commit: Option<String>, new_commit: String }` (sentinel differs from HEAD; old_commit is None on a pre-US-009 DB or fresh branch row). Storage errors when reading the sentinel are warn-logged and treated as 'never scanned', matching the panic-free policy from US-009's `record_branch_scan_complete`. Wired into `run_serve` (in `crates/seshat-cli/src/serve.rs`) at the post-`handle_branch_switch` slot (immediately after `final_branch` is computed). The `sync_root` computation (formerly inlined inside the `if sync_old_branch.is_some()` block) is lifted up so it serves both the freshness check AND the existing background_sync thread. Branch-switch and HEAD-change paths share one boolean: `let needs_sync = sync_old_branch.is_some() || head_change_hint.is_some();`. The old-side hint passed to `background_sync` resolves as `sync_old_branch.clone().or_else(|| head_change_hint.clone())` — branch-switch wins (it carries a `refs/heads/<name>` that gix resolves directly via `try_find_reference`); on a same-branch HEAD change the `last_scanned_commit` SHA works as a commit-ish via the existing `gix::ObjectId::from_hex` fallback in `resolve_branch_tree_paths`, falling through to `fallback_rescan` if the commit is unreachable (e.g. force-pushed). For `is_auto_scan` the freshness check short-circuits to `None` because the auto-scan tokio task below IS the scan — running an additional sync on top would race on the same DB. AC#5 logs (`old_head=<7>`, `new_head=<7>`, branch) are emitted at `info!` from the Stale arm via `chars().take(7).collect::<String>()`; old_head reads `(none)` when there is no recorded sentinel. 4 new unit tests in `git_utils.rs` (parity with the helper-design): `check_branch_freshness_returns_up_to_date_when_sentinel_matches_head`, `check_branch_freshness_returns_stale_when_head_advances`, `check_branch_freshness_returns_stale_with_none_old_commit_when_never_scanned`, `check_branch_freshness_returns_git_unavailable_for_non_git_directory`. New AC-required integration test `crates/seshat-cli/tests/serve_head_change.rs::serve_freshness_gate_returns_stale_when_head_advances_on_same_branch` drives the full pipeline (git init + commit, scan_project, record_branch_scan_complete, assert UpToDate, second commit, assert Stale with old_commit=Some(head1) and new_commit=head2). The integration test pins the gate at the same layer `run_serve` uses to decide whether to spawn `background_sync` — Stale is what flips `needs_sync` to true in production. Spawning `run_serve` end-to-end is impractical (it runs the MCP server until Ctrl+C); broader serve coverage (branch-label change, git-unavailable, progress callbacks) is owned by US-017's `serve_freshness.rs`. cargo build/fmt/clippy/test workspace-wide all green except for the pre-existing flaky `serve_starts_and_shows_startup_info` test in seshat-bin (dangerous-cwd guardrail trips on macOS tmpfs) — confirmed unchanged on clean US-009 commit (574c281) via `git stash` round-trip.

userStories.title: Blocking incremental sync in run_review
userStories.description: As a user, when I run seshat review after changes, the TUI does not open until the DB is up-to-date with the current HEAD.
acceptanceCriteria: run_review reads last_scanned_commit for current branch

If different from git rev-parse HEAD (or last_scanned_commit IS NULL): print Syncing project state to <head[..7]>... to stdout with a spinner if stdout is a TTY (degrade to single-line summary when piped)
Run sync synchronously via a new incremental_sync_blocking function (extracted from background_sync body) with a progress callback that updates Files: X / Y on the same line at 1Hz
Update last_scanned_commit after sync, then open TUI
If git unavailable: skip sync silently, open TUI immediately
Add --no-sync flag for emergency / debug use (per Open Question Q2 default Yes)
Integration test: with stale last_scanned_commit, run review, assert sync ran AND TUI received fresh data
Integration test: progress callback emits at least one update for a non-trivial diff
Integration test: git unavailable → TUI launches without sync, no errors
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Extracted incremental_sync_blocking from background_sync in seshat-cli/src/serve.rs (pub(crate)). The new function takes an optional `Option<&dyn Fn(usize, usize)>` progress callback that fires at the top of every upsert-loop iteration plus a final (total, total) tick (so `continue` paths still advance the UI; the throttle gates only intermediate emits). background_sync becomes a thin wrapper that calls incremental_sync_blocking with None. record_branch_scan_complete (US-009) is preserved at the end of the helper so last_scanned_commit advances in BOTH the serve and the review path. Added `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]` because the helper now has 8 arguments and clippy's default cap is 7 — the alternative (a Param struct) would have been pure ceremony for one extra parameter. New args.rs Review variant carries a `--no-sync` boolean flag (clap derive); lib.rs threads it into review::run_review. review.rs is rewritten: opens via Database::open (so the sync, freshness gate, and TUI all share one Arc<Mutex<Connection>>), pre-flights check_branch_freshness to decide whether to print the syncing line, then calls a new pub helper prepare_review_sync that runs the actual gate + sync. prepare_review_sync returns a ReviewSyncOutcome enum (Skipped | UpToDate | GitUnavailable | Synced { old_commit, new_commit, progress_emits }) so integration tests can lock the gate at the same layer the CLI uses without spawning the (interactive) TUI. The user-visible progress UI is two flavours: a TTY printer that rewrites `\rSyncing project state to <head[..7]>... Files: X / Y    ` at ≈950 ms intervals with a final `done.` line, and a piped printer that emits one line per throttled update (`Syncing project state to <head>: X / Y files`) so CI logs and piped output don't get clobbered by carriage returns. Both share the same throttle const (TTY_PROGRESS_INTERVAL = 950 ms — 950 ms instead of 1000 ms keeps the cadence stable across the boundary). TTY detection uses std::io::IsTerminal on stderr. Tests: 5 new integration tests in crates/seshat-cli/tests/review_freshness.rs cover all 3 AC test cases plus two extras: review_blocks_on_sync_when_head_changed (drives the full git-init + scan + record-sentinel + follow-up-commit pipeline, asserts Synced { old_commit: Some(head1), new_commit: head2, progress_emits >= 1 }, sentinel advances to head2, AND the new util.rs file appears in files_ir post-sync — this is the 'TUI receives fresh data' half of the AC), review_progress_updates_emitted_during_sync (asserts wrapper-counter and user-supplied callback fire on the same events, total is stable across emits, processed never exceeds total), review_handles_git_unavailable_gracefully (no .git dir; outcome is GitUnavailable, callback never fires, sentinel stays NULL), review_no_sync_flag_skips_freshness_gate (sentinel-stale on disk; no_sync=true returns Skipped, callback never fires, sentinel does NOT advance), review_skips_sync_when_head_unchanged (sentinel matches HEAD; outcome is UpToDate, no callback, no sentinel write). Two unit tests in review.rs (prepare_review_sync_returns_skipped_when_no_sync_passed, prepare_review_sync_returns_git_unavailable_for_non_git_directory) lock the smallest gate cases. cargo build, cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace --exclude seshat-bin all green. The pre-existing flaky serve_starts_and_shows_startup_info test in seshat-bin (dangerous-cwd guardrail trips on macOS tmpfs, unrelated to US-011) reproduces on main — confirmed unchanged.

userStories.title: Git-unavailable single-branch fallback verification
userStories.description: As a user, I can run seshat against a non-git directory (e.g., a download, a tmp dir, a git-less project) and decisions still work; freshness checks are quietly skipped.
acceptanceCriteria: detect_branch returns 'main' when no .git is found (existing fallback); document this as the synthetic-branch identity
Freshness comparisons (run_serve and run_review) treat git rev-parse HEAD failure as 'no change detected' — sync NOT triggered, no warnings
All scan paths set last_scanned_commit = NULL when git is unavailable (no synthetic hash)
Decision flow operates as on a single-branch project: all decisions decided on branch='main', all queries scoped to branch='main'
Integration test: scan + review + decide + rescan in a non-git tmp dir → decisions persist, no errors
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Verification checkpoint — every AC behaviour was already shipped by US-001/US-003/US-009/US-010/US-011/US-005/US-008. Audit results: (1) AC#1 detect_branch falls back to 'main' — already implemented in crates/seshat-cli/src/db.rs:303-308 (pub fn detect_branch unwraps get_current_branch() with `tracing::debug!` + .to_string('main')). (2) AC#2 freshness skip on git-unavailable — already implemented in serve.rs:646-648 (run_serve treats `FreshnessCheck::GitUnavailable` and `UpToDate` identically — both yield head_change_hint=None, so needs_sync stays false unless a branch-switch occurred) AND in review.rs:225-234 + 86-88 (run_review's pre-flight match arm and prepare_review_sync's main match both return `ReviewSyncOutcome::GitUnavailable` with a `tracing::debug!` log; no warning, no sync). (3) AC#3 last_scanned_commit=NULL when git unavailable — already implemented via record_branch_scan_complete in seshat-scanner/src/git_utils.rs:59-88 (silent debug-log no-op when get_head_commit returns None; column stays NULL). (4) AC#4 single-branch decisions — apply_review_actions writes Decision rows with decided_on_branch=BranchId(branch_id), and the only branch_id flowing through detect_branch in non-git dirs is 'main', so AC#4 is the natural consequence of AC#1+US-005. (5) AC#5 the integration test — added crates/seshat-cli/tests/git_unavailable_fallback.rs with three tests: detect_branch_falls_back_to_main_when_no_git (locks AC#1 in isolation: tempdir() + assert_eq!(detect_branch, 'main')), full_lifecycle_works_without_git (the omnibus AC#5 test: scan + persist + assert sentinel NULL + assert FreshnessCheck::GitUnavailable + Confirm via apply_review_actions + assert decision row state=Approved/decided_on_branch='main' + rescan + assert decision survives + assert convention NOT re-emitted + assert sentinel STILL NULL), and queries_scoped_to_main_return_results_in_non_git_dir (defensive guard for AC#4: branches.list_branches() contains 'main', node_repo.find_conventions_by_branch('main') returns the auto-detected rows). The scan_and_persist test helper layers record_branch_scan_complete on top of the existing scan/detection/persist pipeline so the test actually exercises the AC#3 invariant (sentinel stays NULL) end-to-end, not just at the helper-isolation layer that scan_records_head.rs already covers. cargo build/fmt/clippy/test --workspace --exclude seshat-bin all green; the pre-existing flaky serve_starts_and_shows_startup_info in seshat-bin (dangerous-cwd guardrail trips on macOS tmpfs, documented in US-009/US-010/US-011 PRD notes) reproduces on clean US-011 commit (f8df2fd) and is unrelated.

userStories.title: seshat decisions list CLI
userStories.description: As a user, I can list all decisions made in this project from the command line.
acceptanceCriteria: New subcommand seshat decisions list [--state approved|rejected|partial|recorded] [--branch <branch>] [--format json|table]
Default format is table: state | hash | description | decided_on_branch | decided_at
--format json outputs an array of Decision JSON objects
No state filter → list all
--branch filters by decided_on_branch
Unit test: empty table, populated table, JSON output is valid JSON
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Added top-level `seshat decisions` subcommand (clap derive Subcommand) with a single `list` operation in `crates/seshat-cli/src/args.rs`. The list operation accepts `--state {approved|rejected|partial|recorded}` (clap ValueEnum DecisionStateFilter), `--branch <BRANCH>` (free-form string matched verbatim against `decisions.decided_on_branch`), and `--format {table|json}` (clap ValueEnum DecisionsListFormat, default table). DecisionStateFilter is CLI-local and converts to seshat_storage::DecisionState via a `From` impl so the storage crate stays free of clap derives. Wired into the dispatch in `crates/seshat-cli/src/lib.rs` as `Command::Decisions { command } => decisions::run_decisions(command)`. New module `crates/seshat-cli/src/decisions.rs`: `run_decisions` matches on the inner DecisionsCommand and currently dispatches to `run_list`. `run_list` resolves the project DB via `db::resolve_project(None, "decisions")` (CWD-based, matching `review`'s shape), opens via `Database::open`, reads via `SqliteDecisionRepository`. State filter pushes down into the repository (`list_by_state` is index-supported by V12's `idx_decisions_state`); branch filter is applied in-memory because (a) the V12 schema has no composite (state, branch) index and (b) decision counts are small (tens to low thousands typical). Output goes to stdout (Unix convention for list commands; pipeable through jq/grep). Table output is a five-column aligned text table matching the AC's column order literally — `state | hash | description | decided_on_branch | decided_at` — with a two-space gutter, dynamic per-column widths sized off the rendered cells (truncated 60-char description with …, 8-char hash prefix, ISO-8601 `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` decided_at via `chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp`), and a trailing-column unpadded line so users can copy-paste rows without trailing whitespace. Empty table returns the friendly single-line message `No decisions recorded.` JSON output uses `serde_json::to_string_pretty` against a local `DecisionJson<'a>` DTO that mirrors the row shape — keyed by `description_hash`, `description`, snake_case enum strings (`as_sql_str()` not PascalCase), `category`/`reason` as nullable strings, full `examples` array (`ExampleEvidence` is already Serialize), `decided_on_branch` flattened from the BranchId newtype, plus `decided_at`/`updated_at` as raw epochs. The DTO is local to the CLI module so the storage crate doesn't need new Serialize derives on Decision and the wire shape is owned by the CLI. 16 unit tests (all green): empty table returns friendly message, populated table includes header + rows + each state value + hash prefix + branch + un-truncated description, long description truncated with …, JSON empty is valid `[]` with trailing newline, JSON populated is valid JSON array (parsed back through serde_json::from_str) with each item containing all 11 expected keys, JSON state strings are SQL-form lowercase (`approved`/`convention`/`rule`) not PascalCase, `load_decisions` empty-DB returns empty Vec, no-filter returns all, state filter (each of the 4 states), branch filter (main / feature/x / unknown), combined state+branch filter (1 match for approved+main, 0 for rejected+main), short_hash boundary cases (<8, =8, >8), truncate_chars boundary cases, format_decided_at against a known epoch (1700000000 → 2023-11-14 22:13:20), DecisionStateFilter → DecisionState round-trip for all four variants. cargo build/fmt/clippy/test all green workspace-wide; `seshat decisions list --help` smoke-tested via cargo run. The pre-existing flaky `serve_starts_and_shows_startup_info` test in seshat-bin (dangerous-cwd guardrail trips on macOS tmpfs, documented in US-009/US-010/US-011/US-012 notes) reproduces unchanged.

userStories.title: seshat decisions forget CLI
userStories.description: As a user, I can remove a decision so that the convention re-enters the review queue on next scan.
acceptanceCriteria: New subcommand seshat decisions forget <hash> [--yes]
Supports lookup by full description_hash or by ambiguity-free prefix (≥4 chars)
Prints the matched decision and prompts 'Forget this decision? [y/N]' unless --yes
On confirmation: DELETE from decisions; subsequent seshat scan + review will re-emit the convention
Errors: hash not found, ambiguous prefix, multiple hashes match
Integration test: forget approved decision → next scan re-emits it
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Implemented `seshat decisions forget <hash> [--yes]` on feat/merge-aware-decisions. DecisionsCommand::Forget added in args.rs; run_forget orchestrates project resolve → open DB → resolve_decision_for_forget (full hash or unambiguous prefix ≥4 chars) → format_decision_summary → prompt_for_confirmation (skipped on --yes) → SqliteDecisionRepository::delete (hard delete, leveraging US-002's idempotent contract). Errors mapped to CliError::InvalidArgument (too-short prefix) and CliError::CommandFailed (no match / ambiguous / db read failure). 16 new unit tests cover resolution paths (full hash, prefix unique, ambiguous, not-found, too-short, too-short-when-unique), summary formatting (full hash + key fields rendered), confirmation prompt (y/yes/Y/YES affirmative; n/empty/unrelated decline), and `forget_decision_with_database` test seam (full hash, prefix, error propagation). Integration test `decisions_forget.rs` pins the AC end-to-end: scan → confirm → assert decision exists → rescan asserts NOT re-emitted (US-008 dedup) → forget → assert hard-deleted → rescan asserts re-emitted. Companion test `forget_decision_resolves_unambiguous_prefix` exercises the 4-char-prefix path. cargo build/fmt/clippy/test --workspace --exclude seshat-bin all green; +18 tests workspace-wide. seshat-bin's pre-existing flaky `serve_starts_and_shows_startup_info` reproduces unchanged (macOS tmpfs + dangerous-cwd guardrail; documented in US-009/US-010/US-011/US-012/US-013 notes).

userStories.title: seshat decisions export and import CLI
userStories.description: As a user, I can back up decisions or share them across machines.
acceptanceCriteria: seshat decisions export <file> writes the decisions table as a JSON array to <file>
seshat decisions import <file> reads the JSON array and UPSERTs into decisions; conflicts: latest decided_at wins (silently); --strict flag fails on any conflict instead
Round-trip test: export → wipe → import → table identical
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Added `seshat decisions export <file>` and `seshat decisions import <file> [--strict]` subcommands on feat/merge-aware-decisions. New DecisionsCommand::Export { file: PathBuf } and DecisionsCommand::Import { file: PathBuf, strict: bool } variants in args.rs (clap derive). decisions.rs gains run_export/run_import dispatch + two public string-level seams: export_decisions_to_string (loads via SqliteDecisionRepository::list, serialises via the existing DecisionJson<'a> DTO from US-013 — same wire shape as `decisions list --format json`, lossless round-trip) and import_decisions_from_str (parses to DecisionJsonOwned via serde_json, converts each row through DecisionState/Nature/Weight::from_sql_str with hash-prefixed error messages, applies conflict policy). Conflict resolution: default = latest decided_at wins (strictly greater overwrites; equals/older skip silently to avoid round-trip churn on unchanged rows); --strict = pre-flight all incoming hashes via SqliteDecisionRepository::get_by_hashes BEFORE any writes, abort with the full conflict list if any exist (no partial writes). New ImportSummary { total, inserted, updated, skipped } returned for tests + CLI summary line. 13 new unit tests in decisions.rs cover: export empty/populated, import into empty DB / empty array no-op / newer wins / older skipped / equal skipped, strict fails on conflict / succeeds with no conflict / lists all conflicts, malformed JSON error / invalid enum string error, round-trip both fresh-DB and in-place-wipe variants, DecisionJsonOwned↔DecisionJson inverse. New integration test crates/seshat-cli/tests/decisions_export_import.rs locks AC #3 end-to-end (scan → confirm 2 conventions → export to JSON file on disk → wipe → re-import → assert table is byte-for-byte identical via Decision PartialEq across every field; second test exercises --strict against a same-DB re-import). cargo build, cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace --exclude seshat-bin all green; smoke-tested `seshat decisions export --help` and `import --help`. The pre-existing flaky serve_starts_and_shows_startup_info in seshat-bin (dangerous-cwd guardrail trips on macOS tmpfs, documented across US-009..US-014 notes) reproduces unchanged.

userStories.title: Cross-branch decisions integration test
userStories.description: As QA, the merge-and-no-reprompt scenario must be locked behind a regression test.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/seshat-cli/tests/cross_branch_decisions.rs

Test approve_on_feature_persists_after_merge_to_main: scan on main, scan on feature, approve convention on feature, simulated merge (move main ref to feature's HEAD), restart scan on main, assert convention NOT in review queue
Test reject_on_feature_persists_after_merge_to_main: same with reject
Test decision_survives_branch_deletion: approve on feature, delete the branch, run scan on main, assert convention NOT in queue, assert decision row still exists
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Implemented on feat/merge-aware-decisions. New file crates/seshat-cli/tests/cross_branch_decisions.rs adds 3 integration tests: approve_on_feature_persists_after_merge_to_main (Confirm flow + ff-only merge), reject_on_feature_persists_after_merge_to_main (Reject flow + snapshot_hash optimistic concurrency), decision_survives_branch_deletion (git branch -D feature, decision row + dedup both survive). Each test stands up a real git repo via tempdir + git init --initial-branch=main, .gitignores seshat.db so per-branch scans don't dirty the working tree, scans on main + feature with their respective BranchIds, drives apply_review_actions on 'feature', simulates merge via git merge --ff-only feature, then asserts find_conventions_by_branch('main') no longer surfaces the decided description and the decisions row is unchanged. cargo test --workspace + clippy + fmt all green; pre-existing flaky serve_starts_and_shows_startup_info in seshat-bin (dangerous-cwd guardrail on macOS tmpfs) is unrelated and documented in earlier notes.

userStories.title: Freshness integration tests
userStories.description: As QA, the serve-detects-HEAD-change and review-blocks-on-sync paths must be locked.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/seshat-cli/tests/serve_freshness.rs
Test serve_detects_branch_label_change_and_syncs (existing path — guard regression)
Test serve_detects_same_branch_head_change_and_syncs
Test serve_skips_sync_when_head_unchanged
Test serve_skips_sync_when_git_unavailable
New file crates/seshat-cli/tests/review_freshness.rs
Test review_blocks_on_sync_when_head_changed
Test review_skips_sync_when_head_unchanged
Test review_progress_updates_emitted_during_sync (mock progress callback)
Test review_handles_git_unavailable_gracefully (no-git tmp dir)
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Implemented on feat/merge-aware-decisions. AC #6-9 (review side) was already shipped by US-011 — `crates/seshat-cli/tests/review_freshness.rs` contains all four mandated review tests (`review_blocks_on_sync_when_head_changed`, `review_skips_sync_when_head_unchanged`, `review_progress_updates_emitted_during_sync`, `review_handles_git_unavailable_gracefully`) plus the bonus `review_no_sync_flag_skips_freshness_gate`; left untouched. AC #1-5 (serve side) was the new deliverable. New file `crates/seshat-cli/tests/serve_freshness.rs` adds 4 integration tests pinning the `run_serve` freshness gate (`needs_sync = sync_old_branch.is_some() || head_change_hint.is_some()` from serve.rs:676) at the same layer the production code uses. The gate primitives are public (`seshat_scanner::check_branch_freshness`, `seshat_cli::db::detect_branch`, `SqliteBranchRepository`); `handle_branch_switch` is private but its outcome (`final_branch`) is modelled in tests as a parameter to a tiny `would_serve_trigger_sync` helper that mirrors the production gate verbatim — including the subtle contract that `head_change_hint = old_commit` (so a `Stale { old_commit: None, .. }` does NOT fire the head-change leg, only the branch-switch leg can fire for a never-scanned branch). The same-branch HEAD test deliberately duplicates `serve_head_change.rs`'s test (US-010's deliverable) — the duplication is the AC-required regression guard so a future refactor that breaks the gate surfaces in both files. `init_repo_on_main` uses `--initial-branch=main` (US-016 learning) and `.gitignore`s `seshat.db` BEFORE the initial commit (US-016 learning, even though only AC#1's branch-switch path needs multi-branch scans, future evolution of these tests is one-line away from triggering the gotcha). Each test pre-flights `!repo.join('.git').exists()` (US-012 learning). `git()` helper captures both stdout and stderr via `Stdio::piped()` so multi-step setup failures are debuggable (US-016 learning). cargo build, cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace --exclude seshat-bin all green; net workspace test count climbs by 4. seshat-bin's pre-existing flaky `serve_starts_and_shows_startup_info` (macOS tmpfs + dangerous-cwd guardrail) reproduces unchanged — documented across US-009..US-016 notes.

userStories.title: README + ADR documentation
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the design and the wipe-DB upgrade path documented so future developers and users understand the change.
acceptanceCriteria: New ADR _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/14-1-merge-aware-decisions.md (or wherever ADRs live) documenting: decision-table-vs-user-node trade-off, why no migration, git-optional fallback semantics, worktree concurrency limitation, future extensions (per-branch overrides, conflict resolution)
README updated with seshat decisions subcommand reference
CHANGELOG entry: 'BREAKING: DB schema redesigned. Existing DBs are incompatible — delete ~/.local/share/seshat/repos/<project>.db and rescan.'
Smoke test doc docs/smoke-tests/merge-aware-decisions.md documenting US-001..US-016 manual verification steps
Markdown lints (if any) pass; no broken internal links
userStories.notes: Implemented on feat/merge-aware-decisions. Documentation-only deliverable (zero source changes). AC #1: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/14-1-merge-aware-decisions.md` documents all five mandated points — D1 (decision-table-vs-user-node trade-off, with side-by-side comparison table), D1 (why no migration — pre-1.0 license to break + the migration would carry forward bugs 4 & 5), D3 (git-optional fallback semantics — synthetic 'main' branch, NULL last_scanned_commit, two-tier log levels), D4 (worktree concurrency limitation — global metadata.current_branch race, scoped vs deferred), D5 (future extensions — per-branch overrides via repurposed decided_on_branch, conflict resolution UI via ImportSummary counters). AC #2: top-level `README.md` created (none existed) with install path, quick-start, and full `seshat decisions` reference for list/forget/export/import subcommands — flag tables and example invocations. AC #3: `CHANGELOG.md` gains an `[Unreleased] — Merge-aware Decisions and DB Freshness` section above [0.1.1] with Breaking / Added / Fixed / Known Limitations subsections; the Breaking subsection's lead line is the AC-mandated 'BREAKING: DB schema redesigned. Existing DBs are incompatible — delete `~/.local/share/seshat/repos/<project>.db` and rescan.' (rephrased into Keep-a-Changelog style but content-equivalent). AC #4: `docs/smoke-tests/merge-aware-decisions.md` walks US-001..US-016 step-by-step with pre-flight, expected outputs, and failure-mode pointers. AC #5: no markdown linter is configured in the repo (no `.markdownlint*` or `.mdformat*` files; `.gitignore` carries no markdown-tooling entries) so 'Markdown lints (if any) pass' is vacuously satisfied; all internal links were verified to resolve via `test -e` against the 15 referenced paths (README → 4 paths; ADR → 6 paths; CHANGELOG → 1 path; smoke-test doc → 4 paths). cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings both pass; cargo test --workspace passes except for the pre-existing `serve_starts_and_shows_startup_info` flake (macOS tmpfs + dangerous-cwd guardrail) which reproduces identically on the same HEAD with the doc files stashed away (verified via `git stash --include-untracked && cargo test ... && git stash pop`).
